acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI_SPEC.md documents both flags and their semantics
- [ ] Example JSON for require-min-seen included
description:
**Problem**
CLI_SPEC.md does not document the read gating flags required by REALTIME_PLAN, which makes the feature discoverability poor for realtime debugging.

**Design**
Update CLI_SPEC.md to describe `--require-min-seen` (JSON Watermarks<Applied>) and `--wait-timeout-ms` semantics, with a short JSON example.

**Files**
- CLI_SPEC.md
title: Document CLI read gating flags

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `bd migrate detect` fails when live remote preview fails and `origin` is configured.
- [ ] `bd migrate to 1 --dry-run` fails on the same live preview error.
- [ ] No preview path falls back to stale tracking refs.
- [ ] Integration coverage pins the broken-origin contract.
description:
**Problem**
`bd migrate detect` and `bd migrate to 1 --dry-run` still collapse live remote preview failures into a local-only view whenever a local store exists. That means a transient auth/network/preview failure can make preview commands report a local-only no-op even though a real execute would fail or make a different ancestry decision once the remote is reachable.

The hard-cutover design locks preview and execute to the same remote-aware source matrix. Preview commands should not silently erase the remote side when live resolution fails.

**Files:** `crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
design:
**Design**
Make preview commands fail closed on live remote preview errors.

- If no `origin` exists, keep the current local-only behavior.
- If `origin` exists and live remote preview fails, surface that error from both `migrate detect` and `migrate to 1 --dry-run`, even if a local store exists.
- Keep stale tracking refs out of the preview path.
- Update the broken-origin tests to assert the error contract rather than a silent local fallback.
title: Detect and dry-run must surface live remote preview errors
